task:
Audit test coverage of /Users/vics/dev/structupath/herdr-browser. Context: Herdr terminal plugin. bin/renderer.mjs (~551 lines) — polling TUI renderer with click-mapping geometry, address bar, escape sanitization, poll backoff, session ownership; scripts/*.sh — open/close/browse/pane/lib; tests/ has renderer.test.mjs, launchers.test.mjs, pattern.test.mjs (57 tests, all passing via `npm test`, node:test runner). Read bin/renderer.mjs and every script and every test file fully. Map which functions/branches are tested vs untested. Identify: (1) high-risk untested paths (geometry edge cases, sanitize completeness, shell script logic tested only via launchers.test?), (2) weak assertions that would pass despite bugs, (3) missing integration-ish tests that are feasible with node:test (e.g. spawning renderer against a stub agent-browser binary on PATH), (4) brittle tests coupled to implementation. Deliver a prioritized list of specific test cases to add (name + what it asserts + which file), and note any production-code seams needed to make them testable. Do NOT edit files — report only.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: attested
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Required evidence: review-findings, residual-risks

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: 16 concrete findings returned with file paths, line numbers, severity, and confidence (T1-T16): e.g. high/T1 tick() passive guard untested at bin/renderer.mjs:290-299; high/T2 navigate() accepts mailto:user@example.com (verified by node probe) at bin/renderer.mjs:434-455; high/T3 shell/JS URL-policy drift at scripts/lib.sh:41-47 vs tests/renderer.test.mjs (verified by execution); weak assertion T13 at tests/launchers.test.mjs; plus 18 prioritized named test cases (P0-P2) with target files, assertions, and required seams.

commandsRun.summary: 57 tests, 57 pass, 0 fail, duration ~1.5s — baseline confirmed before audit

commandsRun.command: bash probe: validate_url 'HTTP://example.com' + browse-pane.sh prefix simulation
commandsRun.summary: Confirmed T3: shell refuses uppercase scheme; browse-pane would mangle to https://HTTP://example.com

commandsRun.command: node probe of navigate() URL parsing for mailto/userinfo/empty-host cases
commandsRun.summary: Confirmed T2: 'mailto:user@example.com' OPENED as https://mailto:user@example.com/ despite comment claiming parse failure

validationOutput: validate_url probe: 'HTTP REFUSED by validate_url'; browse-pane probe: 'https://HTTP://example.com'
navigate probe: mailto:user@example.com -> OPENED; javascript:alert(1) -> REJECTED; http:// -> REJECTED; //evil.com/x -> OPENED
residualRisks: run() poll-loop backoff (idleTicks/sig) unobservable without child-process integration test or a step() seam — P0-2 covers it if implemented
TTY-only paths (raw mode, kitty probe, resize debounce) remain uncoverable by pipe-based node:test without a PTY harness
pattern.test.mjs's Rust-regex↔JS-RegExp equivalence assumption silently breaks if the manifest pattern ever uses lookaround/backreferences
T2 and T3 require production decisions (which URL policy is correct); proposed tests P1-10/P1-11 fail until resolved
childReport.diffSummary: No diff — read-only audit. Reviewed bin/renderer.mjs (551 lines), 6 scripts, 3 test files; delivered 16 findings + 18 prioritized test cases + seam recommendations.
reviewFindings: no blockers (report-only audit; no code changed)
high T1: bin/renderer.mjs:290-299 - tick() passive-viewer invariant (never auto-create session) has zero test coverage
high T2: bin/renderer.mjs:434-455 - navigate() accepts 'mailto:user@example.com' as https userinfo URL, contradicting inline comment; verified, unpinned by tests
high T3: scripts/lib.sh:41-47 vs bin/renderer.mjs:434-455 - validate_url refuses 'HTTP://x' while renderer test asserts acceptance; browse-pane.sh:20-23 mangles it; drift unpinned
high T4: bin/renderer.mjs:475-487 - clickAt guards and eval-coordinate pipeline untested end-to-end
medium T5: scripts/open.sh:14 - HERDR_PLUGIN_CLICKED_URL (primary link-click path) untested
medium T6/T7: bin/renderer.mjs:328-343,495-510 - tick failure lifecycle and selfCreated cleanup ownership untested
low T13: tests/launchers.test.mjs - /closed \d+ pane(s)/ passes with any count; exact count knowable per fixture
childReport.manualNotes: Baseline suite is healthy (57/57). The highest-leverage single addition is P0-2 (child-process integration test against a stub agent-browser on PATH — fully feasible with node:test, no production seam required). Two findings (T2, T3) are production behavior bugs/decisions the test suite should pin once the parent decides intended URL policy; everything else is implementable as tests today using the existing fake-browser and PATH-stub patterns already present in the suite.
